Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 744 upvotes, 75 comments
  2. Caxlsx: Ruby gem for xlsx generation with charts, images, schema validation 60 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. Sending Jabber/XMPP Messages via HTTP 41 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Microsoft Copilot Update Hijacks Default Browser Links 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Show HN: RunAnwhere – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 54 upvotes, 11 comments
  6. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 154 upvotes, 50 comments
  7. PgAdmin 4 9.13 with AI Assistant Panel 68 upvotes, 19 comments
  8. MariaDB innovation: vector index performance 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. Show HN: DD Photos – open-source photo album site generator (Go and SvelteKit) 43 upvotes, 11 comments
  10. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 133 upvotes, 47 comments
  11. Practical Guide to Bare Metal C++ 93 upvotes, 32 comments
  12. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" (2009) 226 upvotes, 96 comments
  13. I used pulsar detection techniques to turn a phone into a watch timegrapher 20 upvotes, 4 comments
  14. I put my whole life into a single database 341 upvotes, 161 comments
  15. Open Weights Isn't Open Training 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  16. Yann LeCun's AI startup raises $1B in Europe's largest ever seed round 378 upvotes, 203 comments
  17. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 329 upvotes, 191 comments
  18. Meta acquires Moltbook 201 upvotes, 122 comments
  19. Amazon is holding a mandatory meeting about AI breaking its systems 245 upvotes, 156 comments
  20. Rebasing in Magit 130 upvotes, 91 comments
  21. More agent tools and AI tools should be pricing on outcomes 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. Show HN: Satellite imagery object detection using text prompts 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. How many options fit into a boolean? 30 upvotes, 16 comments
  24. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 145 upvotes, 111 comments
  25. We are building data breach machines and nobody cares 15 upvotes, 6 comments
  26. A New Version of Our Oracle Solaris Environment for Developers 35 upvotes, 21 comments
  27. Billion-Parameter Theories 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. I built a programming language using Claude Code 30 upvotes, 30 comments
  29. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 32 upvotes, 34 comments
  30. Show HN: A retention mechanic for learning that isn't Duolingo manipulation? 3 upvotes, 1 comments

Last generated 2:00am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
